title: Xylosma tweediana
taxonomy: Xylosma tweediana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae. It is a member of the genus Xylosma, which includes about 100 species of trees and shrubs. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Xylosma tweediana is often used as an ornamental plant in landscaping due to its attractive foliage and ability to thrive in a variety of conditions. The plant's classification is also sometimes referred to as a synonym for Xylosma congesta.
